{{Quote|So let's have no talk of '''leaving''' the planet, Skywalker! I '''need''' you and your droids for my '''war'''-- a war to win '''final control''' of this world once and for '''all!'''|Governor Quarg|<ref name="Star Wars (1977) 13">[[Star Wars (1977) 13|''Star Wars'' (1977) 13]]</ref>}}
'''''Star Wars'' 13''' is the thirteenth issue in [[Marvel Comics]]' [[Star Wars (1977)|''Star Wars'']] series of comics, published on [[April 11]], [[1978]].

==Plot summary==
On [[Drexel/Legends|Drexel]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] decides to join [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Quarg]]'s group in their battle against the [[Dragon Lords]], at least for the time being. Meanwhile aboard [[Crimson Jack/Legends|Crimson Jack's]] space cruiser, [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] is trying to talk his way out of trouble when Jack and his crew realize that they have been duped into coming to Drexel. But Quarg's jamming equipment on the planet's surface causes a disruption on the space cruiser, which is enough of a diversion to allow Solo, [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], and [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] to escape in the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] to the surface below. The ''Falcon'' splashes onto the water covered surface of Drexel, right in the middle of an epic battle between Quarg's men, piloting [[hydra craft]], and the Dragon Lords. One of the hydra craft attacks the ''Falcon'' just as its occupants are exiting from its top hatch. Luke, who is also piloting a hydra craft in the battle, immediately turns on his new comrades and rushes to save the fallen [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]]. He finds only Chewbacca, who, thinking Luke was responsible for the attack, becomes enraged at the young Rebel.

==Release and reception==
Although [[Bob Wiacek]] was uncredited this issue, the letters page in [[Star Wars (1977) 18|''Star Wars'' (1977) 18]] reveals that he did indeed ink two pages in this issue.

==Continuity==
In flashback, Quarg talks about the wreck-and-salvage operation his father used to run back in the days of the Old Republic. In the flashback a number of dead or wounded Jedi are depicted—arguably the first such appearance of Jedi from the pre-Empire period.
